pages.title: DeepWiki-Open Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to DeepWiki-Open: what it does, its two-stack architecture (Next.js frontend + FastAPI backend), key capabilities (wiki generation, RAG-powered Ask, Deep Research, Workshop/Slides), and how to navigate the wiki. Links to all major child sections.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Setup and installation guide covering Docker Compose deployment, local development (Python + Node.js), environment variables (API keys, auth mode, embedder type), and the run.sh / start.sh entrypoints.

pages.title: System Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the full system architecture: how the Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, data pipeline, and LLM providers fit together. Introduces the two-stage wiki generation pipeline and the RAG subsystem. Links to child pages for deeper dives.

pages.title: RAG Engine
pages.purpose: Deep dive into api/rag.py: the RAG class, Memory/DialogTurn conversation state, FAISSRetriever initialization, embedding validation (_validate_and_filter_embeddings), the RAGAnswer schema, and how retrieved context is injected into LLM prompts.

pages.title: Wiki Generation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Overview of the two-stage wiki generation process: structure determination (LLM produces WikiStructure XML) followed by concurrency-controlled per-page content generation. Links to child pages covering the frontend orchestration and streaming chat.

pages.title: Wiki Cache System
pages.purpose: How generated wikis are persisted and retrieved. Covers the server-side file cache (~/.adalflow/wikicache), cache naming conventions, GET/POST/DELETE /api/wiki_cache endpoints, client-side localStorage fallback, and the processed projects listing.
